title: 10 best herbal essential oils for palpitation
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for palpitation are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate irregular heartbeats and associated anxiety. These oils work by promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and regulating the heart rate. The best herbal essential oils for palpitation include Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Lavandula angustifolia, Zingiber officinale, Melissa officinalis, Avena sativa, Ginkgo biloba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cinchona officinalis, and Centaurium erythraea. If left untreated, palpitations can lead to sleep disturbances, fatigue, and decreased quality of life, causing individuals to struggle with daily activities and social interactions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with palpitation because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to reduce feelings of anxiety and stress that often contribute to irregular heartbeats. The oil's sedative effects can also help to slow down a racing heart, promoting a sense of relaxation and tranquility. Additionally, the aroma of English lavender essential oils has been shown to have a direct impact on the nervous system, helping to regulate blood pressure and promote a healthy heart rate.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: English Lavender oil has been shown to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which can contribute to palpitations. This property helps to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
Sedative: The sedative properties of English Lavender oil help to slow down heart rate and reduce palpitation symptoms by promoting a sense of calm and reducing sympathetic nervous system activity.

Cardioprotective: Research suggests that English Lavender oil may have cardioprotective effects, including lowering blood pressure and improving cardiac function, which can help alleviate palpitation symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds that help to calm the nervous system and reduce palpitations.
Buds: Lavender buds are rich in linalool, a compound that has been shown to have a sedative effect on the heart and can help to alleviate palpitation symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried lavender flowers at peak potency and aroma for optimal oil production.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area for several days to reduce moisture content.
Blend 100 grams of dried lavender flowers with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a large glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for 14-21 days to allow for gentle cold infusion and solvent extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard solids to obtain the final essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with palpitation because of its natural ability to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. The oil contains rosmarinic acid, which has a sedative effect on the heart rate, reducing symptoms of anxiety and stress that often contribute to palpitations. Additionally, lemon balm's calming properties help to regulate the body's autonomic functions, including heart rate and blood pressure, leading to a more stable and peaceful heart rhythm. This makes it an effective natural remedy for individuals experiencing irregular heartbeat or palpitation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal lemon balm essential oil has antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to palpitations by calming the nervous system and reducing inflammation.

Calming effects: The essential oil has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate palpitations by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety-related symptoms such as rapid heartbeat.
Cardiovascular benefits: Herbal lemon balm essential oil may help to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure, both of which can contribute to reduced palpitations by regulating heart function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots are used to create a calming and sedative essential oil, which helps to soothe palpitations by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain high concentrations of citral and geraniol, which are known for their ability to calm the nervous system and alleviate palpitation symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in the morning when dew is still present on the plant ensuring high oil content.
Wash the harvested leaves with distilled water and mild dish soap gently scrubbing to remove dirt and debris for 10 minutes.
Dry the washed leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c temperature and 20% humidity for 8 hours or air dry them for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried melissa officinalis leaves in a still with a condenser coil at atmospheric pressure and a steam flow rate of 100 ml per minute.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper into dark glass bottles storing them in a cool place to preserve quality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian essential oils helps with palpitation because of its calming and sedative properties, which can help to slow down a racing heart. The oil contains valerenic acid, a compound that has been shown to have a positive effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety-related palpitations. When applied topically or inhaled, valerian essential oils can also help to calm the mind and body, reducing symptoms of palpitation and promoting a sense of overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic property: Valerian essential oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress-related palpitations by promoting relaxation.
Cardiovascular tonic property: Valerian oil's ability to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation helps to regulate heart rate and rhythm, alleviating palpitation symptoms.
Antispasmodic property: Valerian essential oil's muscle-relaxing properties can help to calm spasms in the heart muscles, reducing palpitations caused by irregular heartbeat or arrhythmias.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for their calming and sedative properties, making them ideal for relieving palpitations.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes contain a high concentration of valeric acid, which has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and treat anxiety-related palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots in early spring or fall for optimal oil quality.
Clean and dry the harvested roots, then cut them into small pieces for distillation.
Set up a still pot filled with 500ml of water and add 250g of dried valeriana officinalis root pieces.
Distill the mixture using steam for 1-2 hours or until the oil yields are satisfactory.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop essential oils helps with palpitation because its calming and soothing properties can effectively reduce anxiety and stress levels, which are often the root causes of palpitations. The oil's ability to promote relaxation and tranquility can help regulate heart rate and rhythm, reducing episodes of rapid or irregular heartbeat. Additionally, maypop's sedative effects can calm an overactive nervous system, leading to a sense of balance and well-being that alleviates palpitation symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Passionflower extracts have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and palpitation symptoms by slowing down rapid heartbeats.
Sedative: The plant's active compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, can induce relaxation and reduce restlessness, making it easier to manage palpitations caused by stress or anxiety.
Cardiotonic: Passionflower extracts have been shown to help regulate blood pressure and heart rate, which can be beneficial in managing palpitations caused by cardiovascular issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, making them effective in soothing palpitations.
Barks: The barks are used for their astringent properties, which help to tone down the nervous system and reduce palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata flowers in the morning when they are fully open.
Dry the harvested flowers using a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours.
Steam-distill the dried flowers using a copper alembic still with 10% water content.
Collect and filter the essential oil after 2 hours of distillation, yielding approximately 1% extract rate.
Store the extracted passiflora incarnata essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort essential oil helps with palpitation because of its calming and sedative properties. The oil contains hyperforin, a natural compound that has been shown to reduce anxiety and stress levels in individuals. When used aromatically or topically, St John's Wort essential oil can help alleviate feelings of panic and unease, promoting relaxation and reducing the frequency and severity of palpitations. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may also help soothe the nervous system and promote overall heart health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St. John's Wort essential oil's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to palpitations caused by cardiovascular issues.

Cardiotonic effects: The oil's cardiotonic properties help to strengthen the heart muscle, improve blood circulation, and regulate heart rhythm, which can alleviate symptoms of palpitations such as arrhythmias and tachycardia.

Adaptogenic and anti-anxiety properties: St. John's Wort essential oil has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which are common triggers for palpitations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain a high concentration of hyperforin, which has been shown to have a positive effect on irregular heartbeats and palpitations.

Leaves: The leaves of the plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in calming palpitation-related symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and stems in the early morning dew.
Clean the plant material by gently brushing off any dirt or debris with a soft brush.
Steam distill the plant material in a clevenger apparatus using 100 ml of water for 2 hours.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distilled water in a sterile glass container.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities or sediment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black pepper essential oils helps with palpitation because it contains a compound called piperine, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. When inhaled or applied topically, piperine can help to relax the nervous system and reduce stress and anxiety, both common triggers of palpitations. Additionally, piperine's warming and stimulating effects can also help to improve circulation and reduce symptoms such as rapid heartbeat and dizziness associated with palpitation.
remedy_properties: Cardioprotective properties: Black pepper's active compound, piperine, has been shown to improve heart health by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can help alleviate palpitations.
Antioxidant properties: Piperine in black pepper has potent antioxidant activity, which helps neutralize free radicals that can contribute to cardiac arrhythmias and palpitations.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Black pepper's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often a contributing factor to palpitation symptoms such as irregular heartbeat.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots of black pepper are valued for their calming and sedative properties, making them effective in reducing palpitations.
Rhyzomes: The underground stem of black pepper, rhyzomes contain aromatic oil that helps to reduce stress and anxiety, alleviating palpitation symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh piper nigrum peppercorns in abundance to ensure sufficient oil production.
Dry the harvested peppercorns in a shaded area for at least 10 days to reduce moisture levels.
Steam distill the dried peppercorns using 500 ml of distilled water and 20 grams of peppercorns for 4 hours.
Collect the obtained essential oil by separating it from the distilled water through decantation and filtering.
Store the extracted piper nigrum essential oil in airtight glass bottles, away from direct sunlight, for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves essential oils helps with palpitation because of its calming and sedative properties, which help to alleviate stress and anxiety. The oil contains eugenol, a compound that has been shown to have a positive effect on the heart rate and blood pressure, reducing palpitations caused by emotional turmoil. Additionally, cloves essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, further contributing to its calming effects on the heart and promoting overall cardiac health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Clove essential oil helps to relax and calm spasms in the heart muscle, reducing palpitation symptoms. This is due to its ability to inhibit the activity of calcium channels, which can contribute to abnormal heartbeat rhythms.

Anti-inflammatory property: Clove essential oil has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the body that may be contributing to palpitations. By decreasing inflammation, clove essential oil can also help alleviate symptoms such as chest tightness and discomfort associated with palpitations.

Cardiovascular protective property: Clove essential oil contains compounds that have been shown to exhibit cardiovascular protective effects, including reducing blood pressure and improving lipid profiles. This may help alleviate palpitation symptoms by regulating heart function and circulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the cloves plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their calming and sedative properties, making them an excellent choice for creating essential oils to alleviate palpitation.
Rhyzomes: Cloves rhyzomes contain high amounts of eugenol, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that can help to reduce palpitation symptoms by calming the nervous system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality syzygium aromaticum leaves and stems from organic farms.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator at 30°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill 100g of dried plant material in a stainless steel apparatus for 4 hours.
Filter the distilled oil through a coffee filter into dark glass bottles containing 10% vodka solution.
Store the essential oil in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with palpitation because of its natural calming and soothing properties. When inhaled, the aroma of ginger oil can help to slow down a rapid heart rate by reducing anxiety and stress levels. The essential fatty acids present in ginger oil also have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to ease tension in the chest area, further contributing to a sense of calmness and reduced palpitations. Regular use of ginger essential oils can promote overall well-being and help alleviate symptoms of palpitation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation and relax the cardiovascular system, which may alleviate palpitation symptoms caused by inflammation or stress.

Cardiovascular stimulant properties: The cardiovascular-stimulating effects of ginger essential oil may help regulate heart rate and blood pressure, reducing the frequency and severity of palpitations.
Antispasmodic properties: Ginger's antispasmodic properties can help relax smooth muscle tissue in the heart, which may alleviate palpitation symptoms caused by spasms or irregular heartbeat.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, rhyzomes are a primary source of ginger's medicinal properties, making them effective for calming palpitations.
Roots: The roots of the ginger plant contain essential oils that help to reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to palpitation relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh ginger roots and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the ginger roots in a warm place for 7-10 days or until they become brittle.
Crush the dried ginger into small pieces using a mortar and pestle and transfer to a glass jar.
Add 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and let it steep for 2 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Nutmeg essential oils helps with palpitation because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce feelings of anxiety and stress that may be contributing to the palpitations. The oil's sedative properties also help to slow down the heart rate and promote relaxation, which can lead to a decrease in palpitations. Additionally, nutmeg essential oils contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the body that may be exacerbating palpitation symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Nutmeg contains antioxidants like vitamin E and beta-carotene, which can help protect the heart from oxidative stress and inflammation, potentially reducing palpitation symptoms.

Cardiac glycoside-like compounds: Nutmeg contains compounds with a similar structure to cardiac glycosides, which are used to treat certain heart conditions. These compounds may help regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations by increasing the force of contraction in the heart muscle.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Nutmeg has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system that may contribute to palpitation symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their calming and sedative properties, roots of the nutmeg plant help alleviate symptoms of palpitation by promoting relaxation.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain a high concentration of medicinal compounds that help regulate heart rate and blood pressure, making them effective in treating palpitation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of myristica fragrans seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground myristica fragrans powder with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for two hours to allow for efficient extraction of essential oils from the seeds.
Allow the mixture to cool and then strain it through a coffee filter paper into another glass container to separate the essential oil from the solids.
Store the extracted myristica fragrans essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with palpitation because of its unique ability to calm the nervous system and regulate blood sugar levels. The oil's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, have a sedative effect that can help alleviate anxiety and stress-related palpitations. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's natural anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the heart, which may contribute to palpitation. By promoting relaxation and regulating blood sugar levels, Ceylon cinnamon essential oils can provide relief from palpitation episodes.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Ceylon Cinnamon essential oil helps relax spasms in the cardiovascular system, reducing palpitations by calming down the irregular heartbeats.

Anti-inflammatory: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation and congestion in the blood vessels, which can contribute to palpitations by improving overall cardiovascular health.
Cardioactive: Ceylon Cinnamon essential oil has been shown to have a direct effect on the heart muscle, helping to regulate its rhythm and contractions, thus alleviating palpitations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help to calm the nervous system, reducing palpitations and promoting relaxation.
Bark: The bark is a good source of cinnamic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate palpitations caused by inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinnamomum verum bark in the morning when it's dry and free of moisture weighing approximately 10 grams.
Dry the harvested cinnamomum verum bark in a warm oven at 50°c for 24 hours to reduce its moisture content to 5%.
Use a steam distillation apparatus and combine the dried cinnamomum verum bark with 1000 milliliters of distilled water to create a 10% w/v solution.
Distill the mixture under vacuum at 40°c for 2 hours, collecting the essential oil as it separates from the hydrosol in a glass container.
Filter the resulting essential oil using activated charcoal and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for further use within 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat palpitation with herbal essential oils is to blend Bergamot oil, known for its calming properties, with Lavender oil, which promotes relaxation. Apply a few drops of the blend to the wrists or temples using gentle massage techniques. Additionally, inhale the aroma through steam inhalation or diffuse it in the air to promote a sense of calmness and reduce palpitation symptoms naturally.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to palpitation that are treated with herbal essential oils are anxiety, stress, and hypertension. Bergamot oil is often used to reduce anxiety and stress-related palpitations, while lavender oil can help calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. Peppermint oil has been shown to help lower blood pressure, which can contribute to palpitation relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils helps with palpitation because it contains valepotriates, which have a sedative and anti-anxiety effect on the body. The oil's calming properties help to slow down an overactive heart rate and promote relaxation, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that can contribute to palpitations. Additionally, valerian root has been traditionally used to regulate heart rhythm and improve sleep quality, further contributing to its potential benefits in managing palpitations and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Helps with palpitations by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which can lead to a calming effect on the nervous system and reduce anxiety-related heart palpitations.
Valeranone: Contributes to the relief of palpitations by inhibiting calcium channels, which can help stabilize heart rhythm and reduce irregular heartbeats.
Bornyl acetate: May help alleviate palpitations by exhibiting a sedative effect, which can calm the nervous system and reduce stress-related heart palpitations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Valeriana officinalis are the most commonly used part for making essential oils due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for its calming effects.
Stems: The stems of Valeriana officinalis are also used to make essential oils, as they contain a higher concentration of sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its sedative properties.

Leaves: The leaves of Valeriana officinalis are sometimes used to make essential oils, although in smaller quantities, as they contain a lower concentration of valerenic acid compared to the roots and stems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of valeriana officinalis roots in the early morning or late afternoon when the plant is at its most potent.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried roots using a copper still at 100-150°c for 3-4 hours.
Collect and separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator or decanter.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or a microfilter to remove any impurities and debris.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oils helps with palpitation because it has a sedative and calming effect on the nervous system. The oil's active compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, work to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which are often triggers for palpitations. By promoting relaxation and reducing heart rate variability, Passiflora incarnata essential oil can help alleviate palpitations and promote a sense of calmness and well-being. It is also known to have a balancing effect on the body's autonomic nervous system.
remedy_constituents: Isophytol: It helps to reduce palpitations by having a sedative effect on the nervous system, which in turn decreases heart rate and blood pressure.
Ferulic acid: It has antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to palpitations, and also has a calming effect on the nervous system.
Isovitalexin B2 (also known as flavonoids): It acts as a calcium channel blocker, which helps to relax the heart muscles and reduce palpitations by regulating heart rate and rhythm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate palpitations.
Flowers: The flowers of Passiflora incarnata contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and other compounds that have anxiolytic and sedative properties, which can help reduce palpitations.
Buds: The buds of Passiflora incarnata contain bioactive compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 pounds of dried passiflora incarnata flowers at 4 pm to capture optimal oil content.
Steam distill the dried flowers in a 10-kg distillation apparatus for 3 hours to release essential oil.
Collect 200 milliliters of passiflora incarnata essential oil from the distillation process after 3 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a 5-micron filter to remove impurities and achieve clarity.
Store the passiflora incarnata essential oil in a dark glass bottle at 4°c to preserve quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with palpitation because of its calming and soothing properties, which can help to regulate the heart rate and reduce feelings of anxiety. The oil's ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels can also contribute to a decrease in palpitation episodes. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help to alleviate tension and strain on the heart, further supporting its potential benefits in managing palpitations. remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with palpitation by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and calming the nervous system.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene helps with palpitation by reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality.
Limonene: This terpene helps with palpitation by reducing anxiety, promoting relaxation, and improving mood, which can help alleviate palpitation symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used to make essential oils for palpitations because they contain calming compounds that help regulate heart rate.
Flowers: Flowers are used to make essential oils for palpitations because they contain linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a calming effect on the nervous system.
Stems: Stems are used to make essential oils for palpitations because they contain sesquiterpenes that help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal oil content.
Steam distill the fresh flowers at a temperature of 100-150°f for 2-3 hours to release the oil.
Collect the distilled water and essential oil from the distillation process and separate them using a separator.
Store the extracted essential oil in a cool dark place in airtight glass containers for up to 6 months.
Use the high-quality essential oil in aromatherapy products or as a natural remedy for relaxation and skin care.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with palpitation because of its natural anti-inflammatory and cardio-protective properties. It contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have been shown to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing heart rate and blood pressure. The oil also exhibits antioxidant and anti-anxiety effects, helping to calm the nervous system and alleviate symptoms of palpitation. By addressing the root causes of palpitation, Zingiber officinale essential oils can provide a natural and effective solution for those experiencing irregular heartbeats.
remedy_constituents: Bisabolol: Helps with palpitation by exerting a sedative effect on the heart, reducing cardiac stress and anxiety.
Gingerol: Assists in alleviating palpitation by inhibiting the release of stress hormones like adrenaline, which can contribute to irregular heartbeats.
Zingiberene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, potentially alleviating palpitation symptoms caused by vasodilation or vasoconstriction.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale for making essential oils due to their high concentration of active compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help to alleviate palpitations.

Buds: Ginger buds are also used for making essential oils as they contain high amounts of zingiberene, a sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to regulate heart rate and alleviate palpitations.

Roots: Ginger roots, although not as commonly used as rhyzomes or buds, are still used for making essential oils due to their rich content of gingerols, which may help to relax the muscles and calm the nervous system, thereby reducing palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3-5 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes with minimal damage during early morning hours.
Steam distill the rhizomes at 100-110°c for 3-4 hours with a distillation ratio of 1:3.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from water using a separatory funnel within 2 hours.
Transfer the essential oil to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dry place for 24 hours.
Analyze the essential oil for its purity and quality using gas chromatography mass spectrometry (gc-ms) equipment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with palpitation because it is known to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to alleviate anxiety-related palpitations. remedy_constituents: Limonene: This terpene helps calm the nervous system and reduces anxiety, which in turn helps alleviate palpitations caused by stress and anxiety.
Oleanolic acid: As a phenolic compound, oleanolic acid has anti-inflammatory and anti-anxiety effects, which can help regulate heart rate and reduce palpitations.
Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-anxiety properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety-induced palpitations by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain the highest concentration of rosmarinic acid and other bioactive compounds that help to calm the nervous system and alleviate palpitations.
Stems: The stems of Melissa officinalis also contain rosmarinic acid, which contributes to their use in essential oil production for treating palpitations and anxiety.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the melissa officinalis flowers and leaves by hand when they are in full bloom at sunrise.
Steam distill the freshly harvested melissa officinalis flowers and leaves in a distillation apparatus at 100 degrees celsius.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water using a condenser and a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove impurities and improve its quality instantly.
Store the filtered melissa officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oils helps with palpitations because of its ability to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. The oil is derived from the oat plant and is known for its soothing and calming properties. It contains a compound called avenanthramides, which has been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress that can contribute to palpitations. remedy_constituents: Lignans: Help to reduce palpitations by stabilizing heart rhythms and improving cardiovascular health.
Saponins: Contribute to reducing palpitations by their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the cardiovascular system.
Flavonoids: Assist in alleviating palpitations by their ability to relax blood vessels, lower blood pressure, and reduce stress on the heart.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Avena sativa are commonly used to make essential oils for palpitation due to their high content of sesquiterpenes, which have a sedative effect.
Seeds: Avena sativa seeds are used for their calming properties and to help regulate heart rate, making them a popular choice for essential oil production.
Leaves: The leaves of Avena sativa contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to reduce anxiety and alleviate palpitations, making them a valuable component of essential oil blends.
remedy_recipe: Harvest avena sativa roots in early spring after the plant has begun to dry and turn brown.
Dry the harvested roots at a temperature not exceeding 40 degrees celsius for 2-3 weeks.
Steam distill the dried roots at a temperature of 100-150 degrees celsius to separate essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with palpitation because it improves blood circulation and reduces inflammation. The oil's antioxidant properties help protect blood vessels from damage, promoting a healthy cardiovascular system. By enhancing oxygen delivery to the heart, Ginkgo biloba reduces the strain on the heart, thereby alleviating palpitations. Additionally, its vasodilatory effect helps lower blood pressure, further contributing to a reduction in palpitations, promoting overall cardiovascular well-being and a sense of calm.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with palpitation by reducing stress and anxiety through their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to a calm cardiovascular system.

Terpenoids (specifically, ginkgolides and bilobalide): Assist in palpitation management by improving blood flow to the heart and preventing platelet-activating factor (PAF) from causing blood vessel constriction.
Bilobalane: Contributes to palpitation relief by inhibiting the release of neurotransmitters that stimulate cardiac activity and promoting relaxation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are the most commonly used part for making essential oils due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to relax the heart and improve blood circulation.
Buds: Ginkgo biloba buds are also used for making essential oils as they contain sesquiterpenes and other compounds that help to reduce palpitations and promote a sense of calm.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used for making essential oils as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help to regulate heart rate and improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in large quantities ideally during the autumn season when the leaves turn yellow.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for approximately 2 weeks or until they reach 10 moisture content.
Steam distill the dried ginkgo biloba leaves using a distillation apparatus with a ratio of 2 parts leaves to 1 part water.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the herbal ginkgo biloba essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with palpitation because it has a soothing effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can cause irregular heartbeats. The anti-inflammatory properties of licorice root essential oil help to calm the body's physiological response to stress, promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation. Additionally, its adaptogenic properties allow the body to adapt to stressful situations, reducing the occurrence of palpitations and promoting overall heart health. This natural remedy can be used as a complementary treatment to manage palpitations.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help reduce palpitations by stabilizing heart rhythms and improving overall cardiovascular health.
Saponins: They may help alleviate palpitations by reducing inflammation and improving circulation, which can help regulate heart function.
Glycyrrhizin: It may help with palpitations by reducing stress and anxiety, which can contribute to irregular heartbeats and palpitations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which is believed to help reduce palpitations by regulating heart rhythm.
Barks: The barks are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of palpitations.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their ability to help regulate blood pressure and heart rate, potentially reducing palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra from a reputable supplier for medicinal use.
Steam distill the dried roots in a still at 100°c for 3 hours to extract the oil.
Collect the extracted oil and separate it from water using a separator or decanter.
Filter the extracted oil through a paper filter or a series of glass wool filters to remove impurities.
Store the filtered oil in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oils helps with palpitation because it contains quinine, a natural compound with anti-arrhythmic properties. This helps regulate heart rhythm and alleviate symptoms of palpitations. The oil's beta-blocking properties also help slow down heart rate, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that can contribute to palpitations. Furthermore, Cinchona officinalis essential oil's vasodilatory properties help improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure, making it an effective natural remedy for palpitations. Its calming effects also promote relaxation and reduce stress.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: It helps regulate heart rhythm and reduce palpitations by acting as a calcium channel blocker and slowing the heart rate.

Cinchonidine: This constituent has a calming effect on the heart, reducing palpitations and irregular heartbeats by exerting a negative inotropic effect (reducing the force of the heart's contractions).
Quinidine: It helps regulate the heart's electrical activity, reducing palpitations and irregular heartbeats by blocking potassium channels and prolonging the duration of the action potential.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for making essential oils due to their high content of quinine, a natural compound that helps regulate heart rhythm.
Barks: Used for making essential oils due to their high content of quinine and other alkaloids that help reduce palpitations and high blood pressure.
Stems: Used for making essential oils due to their ability to provide a balanced mixture of quinine and other compounds that help alleviate palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature cinchona officinalis bark in the early morning hours when the plant is dry and free of moisture.
Dry the harvested bark in a warm dark place with good air circulation for 2 to 3 weeks.
Steam distill the dried bark in a distillation apparatus at a temperature of 100 to 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Collect the obtained essential oil and separate it from the water through decantation or filtration.
Store the extracted cinchona officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centaurium erythraea essential oils helps with palpitation because of its unique properties. It has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can trigger palpitations. The oil's sedative and anti-inflammatory properties also help to regulate heart rhythm and alleviate symptoms of irregular heartbeat. Additionally, its ability to improve blood circulation and lower blood pressure contributes to a more stable heart condition, ultimately providing relief from palpitations and promoting overall cardiovascular well-being.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help calm the nervous system and reduce heart rate, thereby alleviating palpitations.
Phenolic acids: They exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, contributing to palpitation relief.
Flavonoids: These compounds have a positive effect on cardiovascular health by improving blood vessel function and reducing blood pressure, which can help alleviate palpitations.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that help in regulating heart rate and reducing palpitations.
Leaves: The leaves of Centaurium erythraea contain sesquiterpenes and other compounds that have a calming effect on the heart and nervous system, helping to alleviate palpitations.
Roots: The roots of Centaurium erythraea contain iridoid glycosides, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system and can help in reducing palpitations and regulating heart rate.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the centaurium erythraea leaves and flowers at dawn when the dew is still present in the plant material.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area at 30-40°c for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried centaurium erythraea plant material at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to obtain the essential oil.
Filter the distillate to remove any impurities and separate the essential oil from the water phase.
Store the centaurium erythraea essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight and heat sources.
